seo.title: Responsible AI Governance for Agents in AutoGen Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix responsible ai governance for agents in AutoGen agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In AutoGen, Responsible AI Governance for Agents manifests as challenges in controlling multi-agent conversations where agents can enter infinite loops, execute unsafe code, converge on incorrect decisions, or exhaust resources through uncontrolled token usage. These issues arise from AutoGen's flexible agent orchestration patterns like group chats and sequential conversations, where default reply functions lack built-in safeguards. Without governance, agents amplify risks through emergent behaviors in complex multi-turn interactions.

howItManifests.symptom: Agents repeatedly challenge each other's responses without termination
howItManifests.impact: Resource exhaustion, stuck conversations, increased costs

howItManifests.symptom: Agents generate and execute malicious or dangerous code via code_executor
howItManifests.impact: Security vulnerabilities, data corruption, system compromise

howItManifests.symptom: Group consensus forms around hallucinated or biased facts

howItManifests.scenario: Uncontrolled token spending in multi-turn debates
howItManifests.symptom: Debate agents exceed token budgets without convergence
howItManifests.impact: Excessive API costs, timeout failures, scalability issues

detection.name: Conversation Loop Detector
detection.description: Monitors message history for repeated patterns indicating infinite loops

detection.name: Unsafe Code Detector
detection.description: Scans agent-generated code for dangerous operations before execution

detection.name: Token Budget Monitor
detection.description: Tracks cumulative token usage across multi-turn conversations

fixes.name: Loop Termination Reply Filter
fixes.description: AutoGen reply function that terminates conversations detecting loops
fixes.code:
from autogen import register_function

def loop_termination_filter(conversation_history, max_loops=5):
    if len(conversation_history) < 10:
        return None
    
    recent_speakers = [msg['name'] for msg in conversation_history[-8:]]
    if len(set(recent_speakers)) < 2:
        return "CONVERSATION_TERMINATED: Detected potential infinite loop."
    return None

# Register as reply function filter
config_list = [{'model': 'gpt-4', 'api_key': 'your-key'}]
register_function(
    loop_termination_filter,
    caller=autogen.AssistantAgent,
    executor=autogen.UserProxyAgent,
    name="loop_guard"
)

fixes.name: Safe Code Executor Wrapper
fixes.description: Sandboxed code execution for AutoGen code_executor agent

fixes.name: Token Budget Enforcer
fixes.description: Reply filter that enforces token budgets in AutoGen group chats
fixes.code:
class TokenBudgetAgent(autogen.AssistantAgent):
    def __init__(self, budget=5000, *args, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        self.token_budget = budget
        self.tokens_used = 0
    
    def generate_reply(self, messages, sender, config):
        if self.tokens_used > self.token_budget * 0.9:
            return "BUDGET_EXCEEDED: Conversation terminated to respect token limits."
        
        reply = super().generate_reply(messages, sender, config)
        self.tokens_used += len(reply.split()) * 1.3
        return reply

# Usage
guard_agent = TokenBudgetAgent(
    name="TokenGuard",
    llm_config={"config_list": config_list},
    budget=8000
)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Responsible AI Governance for Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly with AutoGen as a reply function filter, automatically detecting governance violations across all agent conversations. By registering Vex's monitoring functions, every agent interaction is scanned for infinite loops, unsafe code, incorrect consensus, and token overspending in real-time. Vex provides structured alerts with remediation recommendations and can automatically terminate problematic conversations.

The integration captures AutoGen's message history and applies specialized detectors trained on multi-agent failure patterns. When issues are detected, Vex can trigger human-in-the-loop review, auto-remediate via safe fallbacks, or enforce conversation termination. This ensures Responsible AI governance at scale without modifying core agent logic.

Vex's AutoGen integration supports both group chats and sequential conversations, providing comprehensive coverage of all conversational patterns while maintaining performance.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex

from autogen import GroupChat, GroupChatManager

# Install: pip install autogen vex-sdk

# Initialize Vex monitor for AutoGen
vex_monitor = vex.Monitor(
    framework="autogen",
    detectors=[
        "agent_loop_detector",
        "unsafe_code_scanner",
        "token_budget_enforcer",
        "consensus_validator"
    ]
)

# Register Vex as reply function filter
@vex_monitor.register_filter()
def vex_governance_filter(messages, sender, config):
    verdict = vex_monitor.scan(messages)
    if verdict.issues:
        vex_monitor.alert(issues=verdict.issues)
        if verdict.severity == "critical":
            return "[VEX_BLOCKED] Governance violation detected."
    return None

# Apply to AutoGen group chat
groupchat = GroupChat(
    agents=[agent1, agent2, agent3],
    messages=[],
    max_round=20
)
manager = GroupChatManager(
    groupchat=groupchat,
    llm_config=config_list,
    reply_filter=vex_governance_filter  # Vex integration point
)

# Vex auto-generates dashboard at https://app.vex.ai/your-team

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install autogen vex-sdk` and register as reply filter to automatically govern your AutoGen agents.
